utterances.text: Welcome back, Lukas and Petra. We are at the three-month mark since go-live, so today is our first proper quarterly business review.
I want to walk through three things: where adoption stands, what is going well, and the open issues we need to clear.
Sounds good. From our side, the overall feedback has been very positive. Our managers love the dashboards and our reps are sending three times more sequences than before.
Numbers back that up. Forty active reps, ninety percent weekly active. Meetings booked per week is up forty percent compared to your baseline before go-live.
The number we are most proud of is that reps complete their training in week one consistently. That was not true with the previous tool.
Now the not-so-good news. We have a mobile issue. Our reps on iPhones cannot edit existing sequence steps without the app crashing on save. This started about three weeks ago.
I opened a support ticket on the twenty-second of April. About a third of our reps work from mobile while travelling and they have had to switch back to laptops.
I have an update on that. The iOS patch shipped two days ago and you should see it in TestFlight. Engineering confirmed it fixes the save crash for the sequence editor.
Good. We will roll the patch out to our reps tomorrow and confirm. Thanks for the fast follow-up.
Other open items: you asked back in onboarding about more granular reporting for individual managers. We released that last month — manager dashboard with team filter, exportable to CSV.
We've started using it. The CSV export is what our regional VP was asking for. Big unblock.
Looking ahead, we'd love your input on the AI Workflow update. We have a closed beta coming up and you've been thoughtful customers — would you be open to participating?
We would. Send the details and we will nominate three reps and one manager to join the beta.
Excellent. I'll send the QBR deck and beta info this afternoon.
